vulnerabilities.title: Spring Cloud Gateway Code Injection (SpEL)
vulnerabilities.description: Spring Cloud Gateway versions prior to 3.1.1 and 3.0.7 are vulnerable to remote code execution via SpEL injection when the Gateway Actuator endpoint is enabled and exposed.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: POST to /actuator/gateway/routes with a crafted route containing SpEL expression in predicates or filters. Then POST to /actuator/gateway/refresh to activate.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Spring Cloud Gateway 3.1.1+ or 3.0.7+. Disable gateway actuator endpoints.

vulnerabilities.title: Spring Cloud Function SpEL Injection RCE
vulnerabilities.description: Spring Cloud Function versions 3.1.6, 3.2.2 and older contain a SpEL injection vulnerability allowing RCE via the spring.cloud.function.routing-expression HTTP header.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Send POST to /functionRouter with header 'spring.cloud.function.routing-expression: T(java.lang.Runtime).getRuntime().exec("command")'
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Spring Cloud Function 3.1.7+ or 3.2.3+

vulnerabilities.title: Spring4Shell - Spring Framework RCE
vulnerabilities.description: Spring Framework versions 5.3.0-5.3.17, 5.2.0-5.2.19 when running on JDK 9+ allow RCE via data binding to class.module.classLoader properties.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Modify class.module.classLoader.resources.context.parent.pipeline.first.* properties to write a JSP webshell via Tomcat AccessLogValve.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Spring Framework 5.3.18+ or 5.2.20+

detection.note: Requires specific conditions: JDK9+, Tomcat, WAR deployment

vulnerabilities.title: Spring Boot Actuator Environment Endpoint - Secret Exposure
vulnerabilities.description: Exposed Spring Boot Actuator /env endpoint reveals environment properties including database credentials, API keys, and other secrets. While Spring Boot 2.x masks sensitive values, the /actuator/env POST endpoint can be used to modify properties, and /actuator/refresh can trigger re-binding.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: GET /actuator/env to enumerate property sources. Some versions expose plaintext secrets. Use eureka.client.serviceUrl.defaultZone property modification for SSRF/credential capture.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Restrict actuator endpoints to management port. Use Spring Security to protect endpoints. Set management.endpoints.web.exposure.include to only health,info.

vulnerabilities.title: Spring Boot Actuator Heap Dump - Memory Secrets Exposure
vulnerabilities.description: Exposed heapdump endpoint allows downloading JVM heap memory, which contains in-memory secrets including session tokens, passwords, encryption keys, and API credentials.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: GET /actuator/heapdump downloads an HPROF file. Use Eclipse MAT or jhat to analyze. Search for strings like 'password', 'secret', 'token', session cookies.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Never expose heapdump endpoint. Remove from management.endpoints.web.exposure.include.

vulnerabilities.title: Spring Boot Actuator Mappings - API Endpoint Discovery
vulnerabilities.description: Exposed mappings endpoint reveals all URL-to-controller mappings including internal/admin endpoints not intended for public access.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: GET /actuator/mappings returns all Spring MVC handler mappings. Parse for admin, internal, debug endpoints.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Restrict actuator endpoints. Do not expose mappings in production.

vulnerabilities.title: Jolokia JMX Remote Code Execution
vulnerabilities.description: Exposed Jolokia endpoint provides JMX access over HTTP, allowing reading system properties, environment variables, and potentially executing arbitrary code via MBean operations.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: 1) List MBeans: GET /jolokia/list. 2) RCE via Logback JNDI: POST to /jolokia/exec with ch.qos.logback reloadByURL pointing to attacker's LDAP/RMI. 3) Read system props: /jolokia/read/java.lang:type=Runtime/SystemProperties
vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable Jolokia endpoint. If needed, configure Jolokia policy to restrict operations.

vulnerabilities.title: Apache Struts2 Remote Code Execution (Content-Type)
vulnerabilities.description: Apache Struts 2.3.x before 2.3.32 and 2.5.x before 2.5.10.1 allow RCE when performing file upload with Jakarta Multipart parser due to OGNL evaluation of Content-Type header.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Send multipart request with crafted Content-Type header containing OGNL expression.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Struts 2.3.32+ or 2.5.10.1+

detection.note: Detect Struts presence first, then check version

vulnerabilities.title: Apache Struts2 File Upload Path Traversal RCE
vulnerabilities.description: Apache Struts 2.0.0 through 6.3.0 vulnerable to path traversal in file upload, allowing attacker to upload files to arbitrary locations and achieve RCE.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Manipulate file upload parameters to traverse path and write webshell.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Struts 6.3.0.2+ or 2.5.33+

detection.note: Requires Struts presence detection and file upload functionality

vulnerabilities.title: WebLogic Console Authentication Bypass
vulnerabilities.description: Oracle WebLogic Server allows unauthenticated attackers to reach the admin console through path traversal using double-encoded dots.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: GET /console/css/%252e%252e%252fconsole.portal to bypass authentication. Chain with CVE-2020-14883 for RCE.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Oracle Critical Patch Update. Restrict console access to management network.

vulnerabilities.title: WebLogic Server Unauth Remote Code Execution
vulnerabilities.description: Oracle WebLogic Server vulnerability allows unauthenticated attacker with network access to compromise the server. Affects T3/IIOP protocols.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Exploit T3/IIOP protocol deserialization or JNDI injection via crafted request to vulnerable endpoints.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Oracle CPU January 2023. Disable T3/IIOP if not needed.

vulnerabilities.title: WebLogic Server T3/IIOP RCE (Patch Bypass)
vulnerabilities.description: Bypass of CVE-2023-21839 fix in Oracle WebLogic Server. Allows unauthenticated RCE via T3/IIOP protocols.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Oracle CPU January 2024.

vulnerabilities.title: Confluence Server Template Injection RCE
vulnerabilities.description: Atlassian Confluence Server template injection vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote code execution. Affects Confluence Data Center and Server 8.0.x through 8.5.3.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Send crafted OGNL expression via template injection in text-inline.vm endpoint.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Confluence 8.5.4 (LTS) or later.

vulnerabilities.title: Confluence Broken Access Control - Admin Account Creation
vulnerabilities.description: Critical broken access control vulnerability allowing unauthenticated attackers to create admin accounts on Confluence Data Center and Server instances.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Access setup endpoints to create new admin account: POST to /setup/setupadministrator.action with admin credentials.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Confluence 8.3.3, 8.4.3, or 8.5.2+.

vulnerabilities.title: Adobe ColdFusion Deserialization RCE
vulnerabilities.description: Adobe ColdFusion 2018 Update 15 and earlier, ColdFusion 2021 Update 5 and earlier vulnerable to deserialization leading to arbitrary code execution.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Craft malicious serialized object and send to ColdFusion deserialization endpoint.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Adobe security update APSB23-25.

vulnerabilities.title: Adobe ColdFusion Access Control Bypass
vulnerabilities.description: Adobe ColdFusion access control bypass allows unauthenticated access to admin endpoints.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Use path traversal variants to bypass authentication checks on CFIDE endpoints.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Adobe security update APSB23-40.

vulnerabilities.title: Apache Tomcat HTTP Request Smuggling
vulnerabilities.description: Apache Tomcat 8.5.0 through 8.5.95, 9.0.0-M1 through 9.0.83, 10.1.0-M1 through 10.1.16 did not correctly parse HTTP trailer headers, enabling request smuggling.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Send crafted HTTP request with malformed trailer headers to smuggle a second request.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Tomcat 8.5.96+, 9.0.84+, or 10.1.17+.

detection.note: Detect Tomcat version first, then check if vulnerable version range

vulnerabilities.title: Apache Tomcat Information Disclosure via Incomplete POST
vulnerabilities.description: Apache Tomcat 8.5.7 through 8.5.63, 9.0.0-M11 through 9.0.43 may return previous request body data in response to incomplete POST requests.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Send incomplete POST request to receive fragments of other users' request bodies.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Tomcat 8.5.64+ or 9.0.44+.

detection.note: Detect Tomcat version, check if in vulnerable range

vulnerabilities.title: Nacos Authentication Bypass
vulnerabilities.description: Nacos before 1.4.1 allows authentication bypass via User-Agent header set to 'Nacos-Server'. This enables reading all configurations which often contain database credentials and API keys.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Add header 'User-Agent: Nacos-Server' to bypass authentication. Then access /nacos/v1/cs/configs to dump all configurations.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Nacos 1.4.1+. Enable authentication. Change default credentials.

vulnerabilities.title: XXL-Job Default Credentials and RCE
vulnerabilities.description: XXL-Job admin interface often deployed with default credentials. Once authenticated, the task scheduler allows arbitrary command execution via GLUE mode (Groovy/Python/Shell scripts).
vulnerabilities.exploitation: 1) Login with default credentials admin/123456. 2) Create new task with GLUE(Shell) mode. 3) Enter command as task script. 4) Trigger task execution.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Change default credentials. Restrict admin access to management network. Disable GLUE script modes if not needed.

vulnerabilities.title: Alibaba Druid Monitor Unauthorized Access
vulnerabilities.description: Alibaba Druid connection pool monitor often exposed without authentication, revealing SQL queries, session information, database connection details, and web URIs.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Access /druid/index.html without authentication. Navigate to SQL monitoring to see all executed SQL queries. Check datasource info for connection strings.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Enable Druid authentication. Restrict access to management network.

vulnerabilities.title: Swagger/OpenAPI Documentation Exposure
vulnerabilities.description: Exposed Swagger/OpenAPI documentation reveals complete API surface including internal endpoints, parameter types, authentication schemes, and data models.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Access Swagger UI to enumerate all API endpoints. Use 'Try it out' feature to test endpoints directly. Extract authentication schemes and internal API paths.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable Swagger in production. Use @Profile("dev") annotation. Configure springdoc.swagger-ui.enabled=false.

vulnerabilities.title: Tomcat Manager Default/Weak Credentials
vulnerabilities.description: Tomcat Manager with default or weak credentials allows deploying WAR files, leading to full server compromise via webshell upload.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Access /manager/html with default credentials (tomcat/tomcat, admin/admin, etc). Deploy malicious WAR file containing webshell.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Change default credentials. Restrict manager to localhost. Use RemoteAddrValve.

vulnerabilities.title: JBoss Invoker Servlet Deserialization RCE
vulnerabilities.description: JBoss Application Server exposes invoker servlets that accept serialized Java objects, allowing remote code execution via deserialization gadgets.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Send crafted serialized Java object to /invoker/JMXInvokerServlet using tools like ysoserial.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove or restrict invoker servlets. Upgrade to WildFly.

vulnerabilities.title: Jenkins Script Console Unauthenticated Access
vulnerabilities.description: Jenkins Script Console allows executing arbitrary Groovy scripts on the server. If accessible without authentication, it provides full RCE.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Access /script and execute Groovy: 'cmd'.execute().text or new ProcessBuilder(['sh','-c','id']).start().text
vulnerabilities.remediation: Enable authentication. Restrict Script Console to admin role. Enable CSRF protection.

vulnerabilities.title: Elasticsearch Unauthenticated Access
vulnerabilities.description: Elasticsearch instances without authentication expose all indexed data, cluster configuration, and potentially allow data modification or deletion.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Access /_cat/indices to list all indices. Use /_search?q=* to dump data. Check /_cluster/settings for sensitive configuration.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Enable X-Pack Security. Configure authentication. Restrict network access.

vulnerabilities.title: Apache Solr Remote Code Execution via ConfigSet
vulnerabilities.description: Apache Solr before 9.4.1 allows RCE through backup/restore APIs used for ConfigSet manipulation.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Upload malicious ConfigSet containing Java code, then trigger execution via core creation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Solr 9.4.1+. Enable authentication.

vulnerabilities.title: Spring Boot Actuator HTTP Trace - Request Data Exposure
vulnerabilities.description: Exposed httptrace/trace endpoint reveals recent HTTP requests and responses including headers, cookies, session tokens, and authentication credentials.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: GET /actuator/httptrace to view recent requests. Extract Authorization headers, session cookies, and other sensitive data from traced requests.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable httptrace endpoint. Remove from exposure list.

vulnerabilities.title: Spring Boot Actuator Logger Level Manipulation
vulnerabilities.description: Exposed loggers endpoint allows changing log levels at runtime. Can be used to enable debug logging to expose sensitive information or to suppress security audit logs.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: POST /actuator/loggers/ROOT with {"configuredLevel":"DEBUG"} to enable debug logging across the application.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Protect loggers endpoint with authentication. Restrict to read-only access.

vulnerabilities.title: Liferay JSONWS Unauthenticated API Access
vulnerabilities.description: Liferay's JSON Web Services API, if exposed without proper authentication, allows enumeration and invocation of internal service methods including user management, file operations, and system configuration.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Access /api/jsonws to discover available services. Invoke methods like /api/jsonws/invoke with service method paths for user enumeration, data access, or SSRF.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Configure jsonws.web.services.security in portal-ext.properties. Restrict API access by role.

vulnerabilities.title: H2 Database Console Exposure - RCE
vulnerabilities.description: H2 Database web console allows executing arbitrary SQL and Java code. Often exposed in Spring Boot applications during development.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Access /h2-console. Use JDBC URL jdbc:h2:mem:testdb or similar. Execute CALL SHELLEXEC('command') or use CREATE ALIAS for Java code execution.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable H2 console in production (spring.h2.console.enabled=false). Never expose to external network.

vulnerabilities.title: GraphiQL/GraphQL Console Exposure
vulnerabilities.description: Exposed GraphiQL IDE allows interactive query construction and execution against the GraphQL API, enabling schema introspection and data exfiltration.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Use introspection query to discover full schema: {__schema{types{name,fields{name,type{name}}}}}. Then construct queries to extract data.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable GraphiQL in production. Disable introspection. Implement proper authorization on resolvers.

vulnerabilities.title: Spring Boot Actuator Shutdown - Denial of Service
vulnerabilities.description: Exposed shutdown endpoint allows unauthenticated users to shut down the application via POST request.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: POST /actuator/shutdown to gracefully shut down the application.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Never expose shutdown endpoint. Ensure management.endpoint.shutdown.enabled=false.

detection.note: GET will return 405 Method Not Allowed if endpoint exists. Do NOT send POST to avoid disruption.

vulnerabilities.title: Apache Tomcat Authentication Bypass via Jakarta Authentication
vulnerabilities.description: Apache Tomcat with Jakarta Authentication (JASPIC) ServerAuthContext implementations that may throw exceptions during authentication process, leading to authentication bypass.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Trigger exception during authentication to bypass access controls.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Tomcat 11.0.1+, 10.1.33+, or 9.0.97+.

vulnerabilities.title: Spring Boot Actuator Session Manipulation
vulnerabilities.description: Exposed sessions endpoint allows listing and deleting user sessions, enabling session hijacking or forced logout attacks.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: GET /actuator/sessions?username=admin to find admin sessions. Use session IDs for hijacking.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not expose sessions endpoint. Protect with strong authentication.

vulnerabilities.title: WebLogic Server Remote Code Execution
vulnerabilities.description: Oracle WebLogic Server vulnerability allows unauthenticated attacker with network access via T3/IIOP to compromise the server.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Oracle CPU April 2024.

vulnerabilities.title: Eureka Service Registry Exposure
vulnerabilities.description: Exposed Eureka service registry reveals all registered microservices with their internal IPs, ports, health check URLs, and metadata.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: GET /eureka/apps to list all registered services. Extract internal hostnames and ports for further targeting.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Restrict Eureka dashboard to management network. Enable authentication.

vulnerabilities.title: Jenkins CLI Arbitrary File Read
vulnerabilities.description: Jenkins <= 2.441 (LTS <= 2.426.2) uses args4j expandAtFiles feature that replaces @filename arguments with file contents. Unauthenticated attackers can read arbitrary files including master.key and credentials.xml.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Send CLI command with @/etc/passwd or @/var/jenkins_home/secrets/master.key argument. File contents returned in error messages.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Jenkins 2.442+ or LTS 2.426.3+. Disable CLI if not needed.

vulnerabilities.title: Apache Struts File Upload Path Traversal RCE (CVE-2023-50164 bypass)
vulnerabilities.description: Incomplete fix for CVE-2023-50164. Struts 2.0.0-2.5.33 and 6.0.0-6.4.0 remain vulnerable to file upload path traversal allowing arbitrary file write and RCE.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Manipulate upload parameters in multipart requests to bypass updated path traversal protections.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Migrate to new FileUploadInterceptor. Upgrade to Struts 6.4.0+.

detection.note: Requires Struts presence and file upload functionality

vulnerabilities.title: Apache Tomcat TOCTOU Race Condition RCE
vulnerabilities.description: Tomcat 11.0.0-M1 to 11.0.1, 10.1.0-M1 to 10.1.33, 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.97 vulnerable to TOCTOU race condition during JSP compilation on case-insensitive file systems (Windows/macOS) allowing RCE.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Upload file with mixed-case JSP extension (.Jsp) that bypasses servlet mapping, race JSP compiler to compile and execute it.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Tomcat 11.0.2+, 10.1.34+, or 9.0.98+.

vulnerabilities.title: Apache Tomcat Partial PUT Deserialization RCE
vulnerabilities.description: Tomcat 11.0.0-M1 to 11.0.2, 10.1.0-M1 to 10.1.34, 9.0.0-M1 to 9.0.98 with write-enabled default servlet and file-based session persistence allow RCE via deserialization of PUT content.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: PUT serialized Java payload to .session file location. Send request referencing that session ID to trigger deserialization.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Tomcat 11.0.3+, 10.1.35+, or 9.0.99+. Keep default servlet readonly=true.

detection.note: Detect Tomcat version, check if PUT is enabled

vulnerabilities.title: Confluence Improper Authorization - Backup Restore Takeover
vulnerabilities.description: Unauthenticated attacker can restore a crafted Confluence backup containing a known admin account, taking over the instance. Used by ransomware groups.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: POST crafted backup ZIP to /json/setup-restore.action without authentication. Log in with known admin credentials from the backup.

vulnerabilities.title: Apache Solr Environment Variable Disclosure
vulnerabilities.description: Solr 6.0.0 before 8.11.3 and 9.0.0 before 9.3.0 expose system properties and environment variables including credentials through admin info endpoints without authentication.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: GET /solr/admin/info/properties or /solr/admin/info/system to read environment variables and Java system properties.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Solr 8.11.3+ or 9.3.0+. Enable authentication.

vulnerabilities.title: Liferay Portal JSONWS Deserialization RCE
vulnerabilities.description: Liferay Portal < 7.2.1 CE GA2 deserializes user-supplied data via JSONWS API without validation. Unauthenticated RCE via crafted JSON payload.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: POST to /api/jsonws/invoke with serialized Java object payload (ysoserial gadget chains).
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Liferay 7.2.1 CE GA2+. Restrict JSONWS access.

vulnerabilities.title: Nacos Default JWT Secret Key Token Forgery
vulnerabilities.description: Nacos 0.1.0-2.2.0 uses a hardcoded default JWT secret key for token signing. Anyone can forge valid admin JWT tokens if the key is not changed.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Generate JWT with {sub:nacos} signed with default key 'SecretKey012345678901234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789'. Use as Bearer token for full admin access.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Change nacos.core.auth.plugin.nacos.token.secret.key to a unique value.

detection.note: Attempt login with forged JWT token

vulnerabilities.title: Adobe ColdFusion Access Control Bypass (CVE-2023-29298 patch bypass)
vulnerabilities.description: Incomplete fix for CVE-2023-29298. Additional path manipulation techniques bypass the updated access controls to reach ColdFusion admin endpoints.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Use modified path traversal payloads that bypass the updated path normalization. Often chained with CVE-2023-26360 for RCE.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Adobe security update APSB23-47.

vulnerabilities.title: Liferay Portal JSON Deserialization RCE (PortletURLAction)
vulnerabilities.description: Liferay Portal 7.1.0 and earlier deserializes user-supplied JSON via JSONFactoryImpl and BeanSerializer, allowing RCE through setter method invocation with C3P0 gadget chains.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: POST to /c/portal/portlet_url with crafted JSON payload containing C3P0 PoolBackedDataSource → WrapperConnectionPoolDataSource → userOverridesAsString with hex-encoded serialized object. WAF bypass: /c;/portal/portlet_url
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Liferay 7.1 CE GA2 (7.1.1+) or later.

vulnerabilities.title: Apache Shiro RememberMe Cookie Deserialization RCE
vulnerabilities.description: Apache Shiro < 1.2.5 uses a hardcoded AES key to encrypt rememberMe cookies. Attackers can forge cookies containing serialized Java objects for RCE. Later versions (CVE-2019-12422) vulnerable to padding oracle attacks.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: 1) Detect Shiro via rememberMe=deleteMe in Set-Cookie response. 2) Generate payload with ysoserial. 3) AES-CBC encrypt with default key 'kPH+bIxk5D2deZiIxcaaaA==' (Base64). 4) Send as rememberMe cookie value.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Shiro 1.2.5+. Configure custom cipher key. Consider upgrading to 1.10+ for latest fixes.

detection.note: Detect via Set-Cookie: rememberMe=deleteMe in response headers after invalid cookie

vulnerabilities.title: Apache OFBiz Pre-Auth RCE via XML-RPC Deserialization
vulnerabilities.description: Apache OFBiz < 18.12.10 includes deprecated XML-RPC library that accepts unauthenticated Java serialized objects, allowing pre-auth RCE.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: POST serialized Java payload (ysoserial gadget chain) to /webtools/control/xmlrpc endpoint without authentication.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to OFBiz 18.12.10+ (XML-RPC removed).

vulnerabilities.title: Apache OFBiz Pre-Auth RCE via Authentication Bypass
vulnerabilities.description: Incomplete fix for CVE-2023-49070. OFBiz < 18.12.11 allows authentication bypass via requirePasswordChange=Y parameter, combined with Groovy expression injection via ProgramExport for RCE.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: 1) Bypass auth: GET /webtools/control/ping?USERNAME=&PASSWORD=Y&requirePasswordChange=Y (returns success). 2) Chain with /webtools/control/ProgramExport for Groovy code execution.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to OFBiz 18.12.11+.

vulnerabilities.title: Apache ActiveMQ OpenWire Protocol RCE
vulnerabilities.description: ActiveMQ < 5.15.16, 5.16.7, 5.17.6, 5.18.3 vulnerable to RCE via ClassPathXmlApplicationContext deserialization in OpenWire protocol (port 61616). Actively exploited by ransomware groups.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Send crafted OpenWire EXCEPTION_RESPONSE packet to port 61616 containing malicious ClassPathXmlApplicationContext pointing to attacker-hosted Spring XML config with ProcessBuilder exec.

detection.note: Actual vuln is on port 61616 (OpenWire), web console helps identify ActiveMQ presence

vulnerabilities.title: Apache Flink File Upload Path Traversal
vulnerabilities.description: Apache Flink 1.5.1-1.11.2 allows uploading files to arbitrary filesystem locations via directory traversal in the /jars/upload endpoint.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: POST multipart file to /jars/upload with path traversal in filename like ../../tmp/malicious.jar
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Flink 1.11.3+ or 1.12.0+.

vulnerabilities.title: Hadoop YARN ResourceManager Unauthenticated RCE
vulnerabilities.description: Hadoop YARN ResourceManager with default configuration (Simple authentication) allows unauthenticated users to submit applications, enabling arbitrary command execution on cluster nodes.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: 1) POST /ws/v1/cluster/apps/new-application to get app ID. 2) POST /ws/v1/cluster/apps with malicious application spec containing command payload in the launch container.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Enable Kerberos authentication. Set hadoop.security.authentication=kerberos. Restrict ResourceManager web UI access.

vulnerabilities.title: Apache Spark Web UI Command Injection via doAs
vulnerabilities.description: Apache Spark UI with ACLs enabled allows command injection via the doAs parameter. The HttpSecurityFilter uses Unix id command to check user permissions, enabling shell injection.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Send GET request with ?doAs=`malicious_command` to inject commands into shell execution via the Unix id command.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Spark 3.1.3+, 3.2.2+, or 3.3.0+.

vulnerabilities.title: Sonatype Nexus Repository Path Traversal (Unauthenticated File Read)
vulnerabilities.description: Nexus Repository 3.x < 3.68.1 allows unauthenticated path traversal to read arbitrary files including system files outside the Nexus installation directory.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Craft URL with encoded traversal sequences to download arbitrary files without authentication.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Nexus Repository 3.68.1+.

vulnerabilities.title: Keycloak Unguarded Admin REST API Endpoints
vulnerabilities.description: Keycloak < 24.0.5 exposes admin REST API endpoints without proper authorization checks, allowing low-privilege users to perform administrative actions.
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Access admin REST endpoints like /auth/admin/realms/{realm}/users or /auth/admin/realms/{realm}/testLDAPConnection with a low-privilege token.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Keycloak 24.0.5+.
